mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in > 50% of instances during early-cycle phases or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year inflation exceeding 2.5% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 55% of triggered months or inflation threshold shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative returns in > 60% of observations or interaction effect vanishes when controlling for macro regime shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) or positive return frequency exceeds 55% during contraction phases.
